reachability_artifacts: cli-run: go test -count=1 -v -run TestModelsCommand ./cmd/sworn/... — drives the registered `models` command's Run function end to end through command.Lookup("models").Run(nil), the exact integration point main.dispatch resolves at runtime (Rule 1), not a leaf internal/model/catalog.go unit test. All 7 providers configured via env vars; the 6 HTTP-based providers redirected to a combined fixture server via a host-routing test Transport (modelsHTTPClient seam in cmd/sworn/models.go); Ollama reached directly via OLLAMA_HOST. Asserts the rendered stdout carries every provider's grouped-by-prefix block with the correct wire-sourced tools annotation (AC-01, AC-02).
cmd/sworn/models_test.go:TestModelsCommand_ProviderFilter — --provider mistral restricts output to exactly one provider through the same cmdModels entrypoint.
cmd/sworn/models_test.go:TestModelsCommand_AllFailedExitsNonZero / TestModelsCommand_PartialFailureExitsZero — AC-03's per-provider isolation and exit-code rule, driven through cmdModels.
cmd/sworn/models_test.go:TestModelsCommand_UnknownProviderFlag — an unsupported --provider value is a usage error (exit 64) rejected before any HTTP call.

effort_complexity.rationale: Known-shape listing per provider + normalisation + CLI command; breadth across ~6 endpoints with canned fixtures.

design_decisions.choice: D1 — catalog.go implements its own no-dispatch credential-presence check against model.ProviderConfig for all 7 providers, rather than reusing internal/driver/registry.Drivers().
options: Reuse internal/driver/registry.Drivers() for availability across all 7 providers — rejected: the registry only enumerates 4 of the 7 (Google/Ollama deliberately unregistered per the registry's own header comment); extending it is out of scope (Registry/resolution changes (S05)).
Uniform catalog-local ProviderConfig credential check for all 7 providers (chosen) — one code path, no split between registry-backed and side-channel availability inside the same command.
design_decisions.human_decision: Type-2 — implementer default, no human ratification required; flagged for Captain design review.
design_decisions.rationale: Registry cannot answer for 2 of the 7 target providers by its own documented design; a single uniform check is simpler and narrower in blast radius than a partial-reuse split.

design_decisions.choice: D2 — Ollama capability lookup makes one /api/show call per listed model (N+1 against the local daemon), the only provider allowlisted for 2 list-shaped paths in the AC-04 transport recorder.
options: Skip per-model /api/show and leave Ollama tools always unknown — simpler, but ignores the wire capability signal spec.json's own rationale names.
Call /api/show per model (chosen) — spec.json rationale explicitly names "/api/show capabilities" as the wire source; local daemon, low latency.
design_decisions.human_decision: Type-2 — implementer default, no human ratification required; flagged for Captain design review.
design_decisions.rationale: Matches the spec's own stated capability source for Ollama; /api/tags alone carries no capability field.

design_decisions.choice: D3 — Ollama is always attempted (no credential gate); a daemon-down failure is a normal AC-03 per-provider error, not a silent skip.
options: Gate Ollama on some presence signal before attempting — no reliable no-dispatch signal exists short of the call itself.
Always attempt (chosen) — mirrors the registry's own claude-cli keyless-availability precedent; also removes the zero-providers-attempted ambiguity for AC-03's exit-code rule.
design_decisions.human_decision: Type-2 — implementer default, no human ratification required; flagged for Captain design review.
design_decisions.rationale: Ollama is a keyless local daemon; the registry treats claude-cli (also keyless) the same way (binary presence, not key presence).

design_decisions.choice: D4 — Google's tools annotation is unconditionally "unknown", never derived from supportedGenerationMethods.
options: Infer tools support from the presence of some supportedGenerationMethods value — rejected: spec.json's rationale states this field is "partial — tool support not explicit", so no wire-derivable Yes/No exists.
Always Unknown (chosen) — fail-closed per AC-02, matches spec intent verbatim.
design_decisions.human_decision: Type-2 — implementer default, no human ratification required; flagged for Captain design review.
design_decisions.rationale: No positively identified marker for tool support exists in Google's models.list response; guessing would violate AC-02's fail-closed rule.

open_deferrals.item: Pricing display (OpenRouter's wire-honest pricing block) — no AC requires it, and PriceForModel is keyed by the registry's fully-resolved provider/model ID, not catalog's raw per-provider wire IDs, so wiring it in would need its own normalisation pass.
open_deferrals.why: Adding an unasked capability-shaped surface risks scope creep against an unwritten AC.
open_deferrals.acknowledgement: Coach-ratified proceed, captain-proceed.md pin 3, 2026-07-11: 'tracking issue FILED as sworn#92 ... cite sworn#92 in proof.json not_delivered.'
